item.title: Trump promised cheaper drugs. Some prices dropped. Many others shot up.
item.description: Since his second term started, President Trump has introduced a flurry of initiatives aimed at taming the excesses of the pharmaceutical industry.

item.title: Blanche says immigrants who committed fraud to become U.S. citizens should worry
item.description: The acting attorney general is touting the administration's stepped-up denaturalization efforts.

item.title: That discount at the pharmacy counter may pack hidden costs
item.description: The new TrumpRx program relies partly on connecting consumers with discount coupons offered by drugmakers. For insured patients, though, using a coupon can prove dicey.

item.title: Hottest U.S. gas prices in years leave drivers feeling burned
item.description: Americans on tight budgets are getting hit especially hard by surging fuel costs. forcing some to make hard choices.

item.title: Trump pardon recipients face congressional probe over "pay-to-play" questions
item.description: Congressional Democrats are investigating clemency recipients who may have obtained favorable treatment from Trump or his advisers "through intermediaries, financial contributions, or other forms of influence."

item.title: Trump threatens Iran strikes, says it's too soon for new direct talks
item.description: Trump threatens Iran with "higher level" strikes if it won't accept a peace deal, but says it's too soon for direct talks after reporting diplomatic progress.

item.title: Rudy Giuliani out of ICU after hospitalization for pneumonia
item.description: Former New York City Mayor Rudy Giuliani has now left the intensive care unit, his spokesperson said, after being hospitalized with pneumonia.

item.title: 5/6: CBS Evening News
item.description: Health officials work to contain deadly hantavirus outbreak on cruise; President Trump threatens Iran with more attacks if it doesn't agree to a deal.

item.title: 3 evacuated from hantavirus cruise as Spain says it will dock in Tenerife
item.description: Three passengers have been evacuated from a cruise ship hit by a hantavirus outbreak, as related cases are confirmed in Switzerland and South Africa.

item.title: How hantavirus may have spread aboard a cruise ship
item.description: A hantavirus outbreak on the MV Hondius cruise ship has killed three people and infected multiple others, officials said.

item.title: Former CNN president describes Ted Turner's "extraordinary" influence
item.description: Ted Turner, a businessman and philanthropist who launched CNN, died on Wednesday at 87. Jonathan Klein, the president of CNN from 2004 to 2010, joins "The Daily Report" to remember Turner's life.

item.title: Trump pauses Project Freedom, calls Iran conflict a "skirmish"
item.description: President Trump touted U.S. actions against Iran on Wednesday. The comments came one day after he paused the Project Freedom initiative to guide ships through the Strait of Hormuz to allow peace negotiations to move forward. CBS News' Holly Williams reports, and Samuel Helfont, an associate professor of strategy and policy at the Naval War College, has more.

item.title: Breaking down Spirit's liquidation process
item.description: A lawyer for bankrupt Spirit Airlines said the rising cost of fuel "engulfed" the airline, drained its liquidity, and derailed its restructuring efforts. There's a sense of urgency to expedite the liquidation process because lawyers say it would benefit creditors and customers. Bob Allen, managing editor of Nomadic Aviation Group, which handles private flight operations for the aircraft leasing industry, joins "The Daily Report" to discuss.

item.title: Jeffrey Epstein's possible suicide note released by judge
item.description: A former cellmate has previously claimed that the note was written by Epstein after a failed suicide attempt less than a month before his death.

item.title: Ted Turner, media tycoon who founded CNN, dies at age 87
item.description: Ted Turner built a media empire that included cable channels CNN, TBS and TNT, and he owned the Atlanta Braves for 20 years.

item.title: Possible Epstein suicide note released
item.description: A judge on Wednesday ordered the release of an apparent suicide note possibly written by the late convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. CBS News has not independently verified the note. Anna Schecter has the latest.

item.title: South Carolina pediatrician hoping to unseat Sen. Lindsey Graham
item.description: Is South Carolina Republican Sen. Lindsey Graham beatable in his bid for reelection? Dr. Annie Andrews, a pediatrician, is running as a Democrat and she thinks so. Andrews joins "The Takeout" to discuss her campaign.

item.title: CBS News Radio flashback: Edward R. Murrow describes bombing raid on Berlin in 1943
item.description: Edward R. Murrow brought CBS News Radio listeners to the front lines of World War II. CBS News' Caitlin Huey-Burns introduces Murrow's famed 1943 broadcast aboard an Allied bombing run over Berlin.

item.title: Rep. Suzan DelBene responds to criticism about DCCC weighing in on contested primaries
item.description: The House Democrats' campaign arm is wading into more highly contested primary races across the country. In a rare rebuke, several House progressives, including Pramila Jayapal and Jamie Raskin, are calling on the Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee to let voters decide. Democratic Rep. Suzan DelBene of Washington State, chair of the DCCC, joins "The Takeout" to respond.

item.title: Todd Blanche says claims of Justice Department politicization are "simply false"
item.description: Critics have expressed concerns about President Trump's use of the Justice Department, including former President Barack Obama during a taped interview on "The Late Show with Stephen Colbert." CBS News immigration correspondent Camilo Montoya-Galvez sat down for an exclusive interview with acting Attorney General Todd Blanche, who defended the Justice Department's actions.

item.title: Food manufacturer Cento is committing "tomato fraud," lawsuit alleges
item.description: In a new lawsuit, two California residents allege that Cento Fine Foods doesn't use real San Marzano tomatoes in one of its products.

item.title: Suspect arrested in Oklahoma party shooting that killed 1, injured 22
item.description: Police said they have arrested an 18-year-old in connection with a shooting Sunday during a party that left one woman dead and 22 other people injured.

item.title: 5/6: The Takeout with Major Garrett
item.description: Trump says war with Iran has "a very good chance of ending"; Indiana GOP lawmakers who defied Trump on redistricting largely falter in primary races.

item.title: Kentucky Derby winner skipping Preakness Stakes, won't make Triple Crown run
item.description: The last time a horse won the Triple Crown was in 2018.

item.title: Ro Khanna says Trump "would have fired" Howard Lutnick if he'd seen his testimony on Epstein
item.description: Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick testified Wednesday before the House Oversight Committee about the Jeffrey Epstein investigation. California Democratic Rep. Ro Khanna called the testimony "embarrassing." CBS News' Taurean Small has the latest.

item.title: iPhone owners could get up to $95 from a new Apple settlement
item.description: A proposed $250 million settlement would compensate millions of consumers who bought the iPhone 16, iPhone 15 Pro or iPhone 15 Pro Max.

item.title: Gas prices top $4.50 a gallon, reaching highest level since July 2022
item.description: Gas prices are now 52% higher than prior to the Iran war, with drivers paying $1.56 more per gallon, data shows.

item.title: Fruit-flavored e-cigarettes for adults OK'd by FDA in major shift under Trump
item.description: The FDA announced its first authorization of fruit-flavored electronic cigarettes intended for adult smokers, a major policy shift after months of appeals to President Trump from the vaping industry.

item.title: U.S. beginning effort to guide stranded ships through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: President Trump announced the U.S. would "guide" ships not involved in the war with Iran out of the Strait of Hormuz. A U.S.-led task force says it has started the operation.

item.title: Donors pitch in to help Nebraska ranchers who lost everything
item.description: The largest wildfire in Nebraska history burned about a thousand square miles of ranchland, including the Wintz's entire 11,000-acre ranch. When they and others faced the threat of losing their livelihoods, thousands of anonymous donors stepped up from across the U.S.
